title: engelhardia mendalomensis
intro: Engelhardia mendalomensis is a species of plant in the family Juglandaceae, commonly referred to as the Engkabang tree, native to Borneo. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The Engkabang tree has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, as well as providing potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
